items.title: Leicester Waterworks Act 1866
items.summary: Local Act authorizing the establishment, operation, and regulation of waterworks infrastructure in Leicester, likely granting powers to a private company for water supply.
items.reason: This 1866 local act is obsolete, superseded by modern water industry frameworks (e.g., Water Industry Act 1991, Ofwat regulation) and privatized utilities. Keeping it imposes unseen costs: legal clutter confusing practitioners, potential for archaic monopoly claims distorting property rights, and administrative burden on courts/archives with zero contribution to current prosperity or liberty, as infrastructure now thrives under competitive institutions.
